notes: With-skill achieved 100% pass rate across all 3 evals (25/25 assertions) vs 84% without skill (21/25)
The skill's biggest impact was on Eval 3 (cache system): 100% vs 75% — caught OCP violation (instanceof) and immutability gap
BigDecimal for money (Item 60) was the discriminating assertion in Eval 2 — with-skill used BigDecimal, without-skill used double
Constructor injection vs method injection was the differentiator in Eval 1 — with-skill used private constructor + factory, baseline used addChannel()
With-skill runs used ~54% more tokens on average, reflecting the time spent reading and applying the skill's guidance
Time delta is minimal (+2.9s average) — the extra tokens didn't significantly slow execution
Several assertions were identified as non-discriminating: try-with-resources is trivially satisfied when persistence is behind an interface, raw types are rarely an issue in modern Java
